# Ruby on Rails Guides

Ruby on Rails Guides are the official, in-depth documentation for Ruby on Rails, a web application development framework written in the
Ruby programming language. The guides are designed to make you immediately productive with Rails, and to help you understand how all 
the pieces fit together. Guides are written in GitHub Flavored

Markdown and published as HTML at https://guides.rubyonrails.org. 

Edge (in-development) guides for the main branch are published at https://edgeguides.rubyonrails.org, and versioned guides for past
releases are available at https://guides.rubyonrails.org/vX.Y/ (for example https://guides.rubyonrails.org/v7.2/).

Ruby is a dynamic, general-purpose programming language. Rails is a model-view-controller (MVC) framework providing default structures for
a database, a web service, and web pages. It encourages and facilitates the use of web standards such as JSON or XML for data transfer, and
HTML, CSS and JavaScript for display and user interfacing. Rails emphasizes the use of well-known software engineering patterns and
paradigms, including convention over configuration (CoC), don't repeat yourself (DRY), and the active record pattern.

The guides below assume a working knowledge of Ruby and Rails and are not intended as a replacement for the API documentation at
https://api.rubyonrails.org. Guides marked "work in progress" are incomplete drafts.

## Start Here

- [Getting Started with Rails](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/getting_started.html): Everything you need to know to install Rails and create your first application.
- [Install Ruby on Rails](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/install_ruby_on_rails.html): Learn how to install the Ruby programming language and Ruby on Rails.

## Models

- [Active Record Basics](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_basics.html): How models interact with the application's database.
- [Active Record Migrations](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_migrations.html): Evolving your database schema over time using a Ruby DSL.
- [Active Record Validations](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_validations.html): Validating the state of objects before they go into the database.
- [Active Record Callbacks](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_callbacks.html): Hooking into the life cycle of Active Record objects.
- [Active Record Associations](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/association_basics.html): Connections between two Active Record models.
- [Active Record Query Interface](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_querying.html): Retrieving data from the database using Active Record.
- [Active Model Basics](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_model_basics.html): Plain Ruby objects with Action Pack integration, without database persistence.

## Views

- [Action View Overview](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/action_view_overview.html): Generating HTML for web responses.
- [Layouts and Rendering in Rails](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/layouts_and_rendering.html): Rendering, redirecting, content_for blocks, and partials.
- [Action View Helpers](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/action_view_helpers.html): Common helpers for dates, links, images, sanitizing and localizing content.
- [Action View Form Helpers](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/form_helpers.html): Generating form markup without the tedium of handling naming and attributes by hand.

## Controllers

- [Action Controller Overview](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/action_controller_overview.html): How controllers work and fit into the request cycle.
- [Action Controller Advanced Topics](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/action_controller_advanced_topics.html): CSRF protection, HTTP authentication, streaming, exceptions, log filtering.
- [Rails Routing from the Outside In](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/routing.html): How the Rails router recognizes URLs and dispatches to controller actions.

## Other Components

- [Active Support Core Extensions](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_support_core_extensions.html): Ruby language extensions and utilities used throughout Rails.
- [Action Mailer Basics](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/action_mailer_basics.html): Sending emails from your application.
- [Action Mailbox Basics](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/action_mailbox_basics.html): Receiving emails with Action Mailbox.
- [Action Text Overview](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/action_text_overview.html): Handling rich text content with Action Text.
- [Active Job Basics](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_job_basics.html): Declaring and running background jobs on various queuing backends.
- [Active Storage Overview](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_storage_overview.html): Uploading files to cloud storage and attaching them to Active Record models.
- [Action Cable Overview](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/action_cable_overview.html): Integrating WebSockets with a Rails application for real-time features.

## Digging Deeper

- [Testing Rails Applications](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/testing.html): Writing and configuring tests in Rails.
- [Debugging Rails Applications](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/debugging_rails_applications.html): Techniques for debugging Rails applications.
- [Configuring Rails Applications](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/configuring.html): Basic configuration settings for a Rails application.
- [The Rails Command Line](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/command_line.html): Command line tools used in everyday Rails development.
- [The Asset Pipeline](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/asset_pipeline.html): Managing assets with Propshaft.
- [Working with JavaScript in Rails](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/working_with_javascript_in_rails.html): Import maps, jsbundling-rails, and Turbo basics.
- [Internationalization (I18n)](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/i18n.html): Translating content, pluralization, date formats, and more.
- [The Rails Initialization Process](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/initialization.html): An in-depth look at Rails startup internals. (Work in progress)
- [Autoloading and Reloading](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/autoloading_and_reloading_constants.html): How autoloading and reloading constants work.
- [Active Support Instrumentation](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_support_instrumentation.html): Measuring events inside Rails with the instrumentation API. (Work in progress)
- [Using Rails for API-only Applications](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/api_app.html): Building a JSON API application with Rails.

## Going to Production

- [Tuning Performance for Deployment](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/tuning_performance_for_deployment.html): Performance and concurrency configuration for production.
- [Caching with Rails](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/caching_with_rails.html): Speeding up a Rails application with caching.
- [Securing Rails Applications](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/security.html): Common security problems in web applications and how Rails helps avoid them.
- [Error Reporting in Rails Applications](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/error_reporting.html): Managing errors that occur in Rails applications.

## Advanced Active Record

- [Active Record and PostgreSQL](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_postgresql.html): PostgreSQL-specific usage of Active Record. (Work in progress)
- [Multiple Databases](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_multiple_databases.html): Using multiple databases in your application.
- [Active Record Encryption](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_encryption.html): Encrypting database information using Active Record.
- [Composite Primary Keys](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/active_record_composite_primary_keys.html): Composite primary keys for database tables.

## Extending Rails

- [The Basics of Creating Rails Plugins](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/plugins.html): Building a plugin to extend Rails functionality.
- [Rails on Rack](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/rails_on_rack.html): Rails integration with Rack and other Rack components.
- [Creating and Customizing Rails Generators & Templates](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/generators.html): Adding generators or customizing built-in ones.
- [Rails Engines Overview](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/engines.html): Building miniature applications that extend a host application.
- [Threading and Code Execution in Rails](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/threading_and_code_execution.html): Working with concurrency in a Rails application.

## Contributing

- [Contributing to Ruby on Rails](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/contributing_to_ruby_on_rails.html): Ways to get involved in the ongoing development of Rails.
- [API Documentation Guidelines](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/api_documentation_guidelines.html): Guidelines for writing Rails API documentation.
- [Guides Guidelines](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/ruby_on_rails_guides_guidelines.html): Guidelines for writing these guides.
- [Installing Rails Core Development Dependencies](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/development_dependencies_install.html): Setting up an environment for Rails core development.

## Policies

- [Maintenance Policy](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/maintenance_policy.html): Which versions of Rails are currently supported.

## Release Notes

- [Upgrading Ruby on Rails](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/upgrading_ruby_on_rails.html): Steps to follow when upgrading to a newer version of Rails.
- Version-specific release notes (e.g. [8.1](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/8_1_release_notes.html), [8.0](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/8_0_release_notes.html), [7.2](https://guides.rubyonrails.org/7_2_release_notes.html)) are linked from the main guides index at https://guides.rubyonrails.org/.

## Optional

- [Ruby on Rails API](https://api.rubyonrails.org/): Full class and method reference documentation for the Rails framework.
- [Edge Guides](https://edgeguides.rubyonrails.org/): Guides for the unreleased, in-development version of Rails on the main branch.
- [Rails Blog](https://rubyonrails.org/blog): News and announcements about Ruby on Rails.
- [Rails Discussion Forum](https://discuss.rubyonrails.org/): Community discussion and support forum.
